rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is causal estimation of paid subscription effects and a sunk-cost mechanism, whereas the cited paper studies ethnic salience, social-status distinctiveness, and spokesperson-ethnicity advertising effects in South Africa—constructs that are not evidently tied to the subscription-program design. The only linkage provided is a single DOI-matched reference with no in-text citation context, suggesting at most a broad/background mention rather than a likely substantive methodological or theoretical dependency.
